content.plain_english: This week in Year 4 maths, your child is working on Decimals A. The learning focuses on: solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why; estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence; and measure and calculate the perimeter of a rectilinear figure (including squares) in centimetres and metres. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children begin by folding paper, using fraction bars, and drawing bar models to see the concept visually before working with numbers and symbols. Discussion in pairs and small groups helps them explain and justify their reasoning using precise mathematical vocabulary.
content.home_activity: Look at price labels in the kitchen — tins, packets, or bottles. Practise reading decimal amounts aloud and round prices to the nearest pound. Estimate the total cost of a few items — this puts decimals in a real, meaningful context.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to solve addition and subtraction two-step problems in contexts, deciding which operations and methods to use and why?
Was it easy or tricky to estimate, compare and calculate different measures, including money in pounds and pence? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: The dot separating the whole number part from the fractional part — e.g. 3.7.
The first digit after the decimal point — each tenth is one part out of ten.
The second digit after the decimal point — one part out of a hundred.
Having the same value — e.g. 0.5 = ½ = 50%.
Give a decimal to a certain number of decimal places.

content.times_table_tip: Year 4 children should know all their times tables to 12 × 12 by the end of the year — the national Multiplication Tables Check happens in Year 4. Daily 3-minute practice mixing tables in random order is the best preparation.
